addressed: yaml.Loader usage in load_configs_from_directory() replaced with yaml.safe_load()
structured_output.reasoning: The finding is valid. load_configs_from_directory() in superset/examples/utils.py used yaml.Loader (with a # noqa: S506 suppression) to parse metadata.yaml from bundled example directories. yaml.Loader allows arbitrary Python object instantiation via constructor tags like !!python/object/apply:. The metadata only contains simple scalars (version, timestamp, type) per MetadataSchema, so yaml.safe_load() is fully sufficient and consistent with the rest of the codebase (load_yaml() in superset/commands/importers/v1/utils.py and data_loading.py both use yaml.safe_load). The fix replaces yaml.Loader with yaml.safe_load() and removes the noqa suppression. Two regression tests were added: one proving unsafe YAML constructors are now rejected, and one verifying the type-stripping logic still works correctly.
